data.title: Salesforce likely to offer up 'strong' results, guidance: Wedbush
data.description:
Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) is set to report fiscal third-quarter results tomorrow after the close of trading, and Wedbush Securities is expecting a "strong" set of results and guidance from the Marc Benioff-led company.

"Our recent field checks indicate that more customers are seeing elevated use cases for CRM’s entire product portfolio while advancing into its next wave of growth and capitalizing on the transformational demand for AI solutions by increasing automation and efficiencies across organizations," analyst Dan Ives wrote in a note to clients. "We continue to believe the risk/ reward on CRM is very compelling at current levels as the Agentforce strategy plays out over the coming years, while the installed base opportunity is still underappreciated by the Street."

Ives maintained his Outperform rating and $375 price target on Salesforce, and added that the stock is one of the firm's "favorite tech names heading into 2025."

The introduction of Agentforce by Salesforce in September has helped usher in added confidence that the company is monetizing artificial intelligence, with Ives suggesting that the company could see a boost of $4B in annual revenue by 2025, based on estimates and field work.

"While this will not happen overnight, the AI monetization story at CRM will be a key growth catalyst for the tech stalwart over the coming years with Agentforce representing the company's latest advancements in the AI space with AI agents built with the latest of AI capabilities to train these models that handle routine use cases, so humans can focus on handling more complex situations with significantly higher resolution on sales and services issues," Ives added.

As such, Salesforce's AI initiatives could be worth roughly $40 per share as the company's monetization efforts take shape over the next 12 to 18 months.

A consensus of analysts expect Salesforce to earn $2.45 per share on $9.35B in revenue.

data.title: Angel Wing Metals announces increase in private placement offering to $2M
data.description:
• Angel Wing Metals (OTCPK:HEXPF) announced its intention to increase the size of the previously announced 20M non-brokered private placement to 40M, for gross proceeds up to $2M.
  • The offering is priced at $0.05 each, with each unit consisting of one common share and one-half common share purchase warrant. Two half warrants comprise one common share purchase warrant of the company.
  • Each warrant will be exercisable at a price of $0.10 per share, exercisable for a period of 24 months.
  • The proceeds from the unit offering will be used for diamond drilling at its La Reyna project in Mexico and for general and corporate purposes.
  • The securities issued in the unit offering contain a statutory four-month plus one-day hold period.
  • The closing of the offering is expected on or about December 23, 2024.

data.title: Bitfarms generates 48% lower bitcoins in November
data.description:
• Bitcoin data center operations provider Bitfarms (NASDAQ:BITF) generated 204 bitcoins in November, down 13.56% from 236 in October and 47.96% from November 2023.
  • BITF shares were trading -0.47% Monday pre-market at $2.14.
  • The month-end operating hashrate stood at 12.8 EH/s.
  • The company sold 171 bitcoins during the month, compared to 194 in the prior month and 350 a year ago.
  • Bitfarms upgraded nearly 19,000 T21 miners to S21 Pro miners with delivery anticipated in December and January.
  • Additionally, the company said it is expanding its board to six members from five, with the shareholders approval received at the Nov. 20 special meeting.
  • Also, Benoit Gobeil has resigned as chief infrastructure officer, effective November-end.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: MARA mines 26.7% more bitcoin in November
data.description:
• MARA (NASDAQ:MARA) said in November the firm mined 26.7% more bitcoin (BTC-USD) as compared to the last month.
  • The firm said average BTC produced per day was 30.7.
  • Energized hash rate rose 15% to 46.1 EH/s.
  • "Overall, we now hold a total of 34,959 BTC, valued at $3.3 billion based on a spot price of $95,000 per BTC," Fred Thiel, MARA's chairman and CEO, said.
  • Shares were down 1.6% in premarket trade.

data.title: OpenAI looks to add advertising to AI products, CFO tells FT
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)-backed generative AI company OpenAI is discussing plans to introduce advertising to its range of products, as the ChatGPT maker seeks new revenue sources, chief financial officer Sarah Friar told the Financial Times.

The San Francisco-based group, which in October secured $6.6B in new funding, has been hiring advertising talent from big tech rivals such as Meta (META) and Google (GOOGL), the report said, citing multiple people familiar with the matter.

In a statement following the interview, Friar added: "Our current business is experiencing rapid growth and we see significant opportunities within our existing business model. While we’re open to exploring other revenue streams in the future, we have no active plans to pursue advertising.”

Its smaller rival Perplexity is already piloting advertising in its AI-powered search engine. Friar, who previously held leadership roles at companies such as Nextdoor, Square and Salesforce, pointed to the wealth of advertising experience between herself and the company’s chief product officer Kevin Weil.

Elon Musk, meanwhile, on Saturday filed for a preliminary injunction against OpenAI, to prevent the company from engaging in what Musk's lawyers claim is anticompetitive behavior.

In other news, OpenAI has reportedly considered launching its own web browser to challenge Google Chrome (GOOG), which has dominated the search space for more than a decade.

A group of Canadian news media companies have also sued OpenAI for illegally using their content to train its artificial intelligence models.

Sam Altman-led OpenAI became the second-highest valued startup in the world in October, after completing its latest fundraising round of $6.6 billion, propelling the company to a valuation of $157 billion.

data.title: TreeHouse Foods to buy Harris Tea's private brand business for $205M
data.description:
• TreeHouse Foods (NYSE:THS) is set to acquire Harris Tea's private brand tea business, a leading private brand tea manufacturer in the U.S. for $205 million.
  • The addition includes manufacturing facilities in Moorestown, NJ and Marietta, GA, and approximately 300 employees.
  • The development will provide vertical integration across the company's existing tea business, and will be accretive to the company's overall growth and margins.
  • The purchase price represents 8.5x trailing-twelve-month adjusted EBITDA, or 6.5x net of synergies and tax benefits.
  • Upon closing, the company expects its leverage ratio to be within its target range of 3.0x to 3.5x.
  • The transaction, expected to close in Q1 2025, will be funded primarily with cash on hand.
  • The company reiterates its 2024 guidance, expecting sequential improvement in volume growth and profit margin in Q4.

data.title: Vitalhub amends existing credit facilities
data.description:
• Vitalhub (OTCQX:VHIBF) said it has amended and expanded its existing credit facilities with The Bank of Nova Scotia.
  • Under the new terms, the company's total committed borrowing capacity has increased to $65 million from $33 million.
  • The amended credit facilities consist of a $60 million term facility and a $5 million revolving facility.

data.title: Battery X Metals to execute 3.3:1 share consolidation
data.description:
• Battery X Metals (OTCQB:BATXF) said it will consolidate its outstanding common shares on the basis of 3.3 existing shares for 1 new share.
  • The company’s common shares will begin trading on the exchange on a consolidated basis on December 10, 2024.
  • Post consolidation, the common shares will be reduced from 70.93M shares issued and outstanding, and after the consolidation, it is expected to have ~21.49M shares issued and outstanding, subject to rounding.
  • The company's board believes the consolidation will enhance its flexibility for future corporate activities, market the common shares more effectively, and attract a wider range of potential investors, thereby securing additional financing for operational and growth initiatives.
  • The company will be obtaining a new CUSIP and ISIN number in connection with the consolidation.

data.title: Core Scientific stock slips on $500M convertible debt offering
data.description:
Core Scientific (NASDAQ:CORZ) has announced a private offering of $500M aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2031.

The company also expects to grant the initial purchasers of the notes a 13-day option to purchase up to an additional $75M principal amount of notes.

The notes will accrue interest payable semi-annually in arrears and mature on June 15, 2031 unless earlier converted, redeemed or repurchased.

The interest rate, initial conversion rate and other terms of the notes will be determined at the pricing of the offering.

Core Scientific (CORZ) plans to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including working capital, operating expenses, capital expenditures, acquisitions of complementary businesses, or repurchases of its securities.

Shares were down 6.8% premarket on Monday.

data.title: No UN plastic treaty as oil producers oppose plastic production caps
data.description:
Negotiations for a legally binding global treaty on plastic pollution have failed to reach an agreement in Busan, South Korea, as some countries opposed limiting new plastic production and phasing out chemicals of concern. Talks are expected to reconvene in 2025.

This marks the third United Nations forum in the past month to end with no agreement on the plastic treaty, which would've been one of the most significant since the 2015 Paris Agreement.

"Negotiators reached a greater degree of convergence on the structure and elements of the treaty text," said Inger Andersen, executive director of the UN Environment Program. "But it is clear there is persisting divergence in critical areas and more time is needed for these areas to be addressed."

The negotiations saw 100 countries supporting limits on new plastic production, and 140 nations backing an obligation to phase out problematic plastic products.

However, some oil producing nations - including Saudi Arabia and Russia - strongly opposed capping plastic production and instead called for targeting plastic waste. Another issue was financing from developed countries to help developing nations implement the treaty.

"If it wasn't for Saudi and Russia, we would have reached an agreement here," a European negotiator told the Financial Times. "Quite a bit of progress has been achieved."

The latest draft of the treaty "keeps production reduction on the table, but is also littered with concessions to petrochemical states," the Global Alliance for Incinerator Alternatives said.

Annual global plastic production is projected to reach about 450M tons by 2025, according to UNEP's Civil Society Unit. The plastics sector is also a major driver of fossil fuel demand and greenhouse gas emissions.
